mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 280.0 trigger immediate Fed hawkishness and consumer spending deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 280.0, or signal loses predictive power after 2022 Q2.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent low unemployment and robust GDP growth indicate a strong economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply chain bottlenecks and weakening demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand that directly boosts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and rising policy rates creates a stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or joint Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 300,000 in a single month, labor market tightness drives wage inflation and margin pressure, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where payroll growth exceeds 300k, or signal Rank IC is < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a rate-hiking cycle signals broader credit tightening and reduced consumer leverage, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during tightening regimes, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.
